home.title: Virtual Medical Laboratory
home.tagline: Choose a lab. Complete each experiment step by step — practise or take the exam.
home.footer: All {done} labs are ready to use

1.title: Bacterial cell morphology
1.subtitle: Simple stain — methylene blue
1.desc: Prepare a smear, fix it and stain with methylene blue to study the cell shape under the microscope.

2.title: Gram staining method
2.subtitle: Differential staining
2.desc: Use crystal violet, Lugol, alcohol and fuchsin to separate Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.

3.title: Drigalski plating
3.subtitle: Spreader inoculation — pure culture
3.desc: Spread the suspension across 3 plates in turn to isolate single colonies and obtain a pure culture, then Gram stain.

4.title: Antibiotic sensitivity
4.subtitle: Paper-disk (disk-diffusion) method
4.desc: Lawn-inoculate the culture, place antibiotic disks, measure the zones of inhibition and determine sensitivity.

5.title: Studying living microorganisms
5.subtitle: «Crushed-drop» preparation
5.desc: Prepare a crushed-drop mount of living bacteria in saline, cover it with a cover slip and observe motility at ×40.

6.title: Ziehl-Neelsen method
6.subtitle: Acid-fast staining
6.desc: Detect the tuberculosis bacillus (AFB) with carbol fuchsin, acid and methylene blue.

ui.learnDesc: Guidance and the next tool are shown at every step. No score — practise freely.
ui.examDescPdf: First arrange the procedure, then perform it unaided. Scored by the PDF criteria (100 total).
ui.examDescStages: First arrange the procedure, then perform it unaided. Scored by stages (100 total).
ui.planInstruction: Place the steps in the correct order.
ui.planLocked: Once confirmed, the order can't be changed.
ui.planFirst: First arrange the procedure in the right panel →
ui.dragHintSidebar: Drag the tools onto the bench and place them anywhere
ui.dragToTable: Drag the tools from the left panel onto the bench
ui.examBanner: Exam — no help. Do it as best you can.

hint.degrease: Pass the slide through the spirit-lamp flame to degrease it
hint.saline: Place a drop of physiological saline (NaCl) on the centre of the slide
hint.flame: Sterilise the inoculation loop in the flame
hint.charge: With the cooled loop, lightly touch and take culture
hint.mix: Mix the culture into the drop until even
hint.cover: Cover the drop with the cover slip (lower it on edge)
hint.blot: Blot the excess fluid with filter paper
hint.observe: Take the prep to the microscope and view it with the ×40 objective

toast.needBridge: First place the glass bridge on the bench
toast.needPrep: First prepare the slide and cover it with the cover slip
toast.success: ✓ The prep is ready — you completed the task successfully!

steps.degreaseResult: Clean the slide and degrease it by passing it through the spirit-lamp flame
steps.salineResult: Place a drop of sterile physiological saline on the centre of the slide
steps.loopResult: Flame the loop, cool it and lightly take culture
steps.mixResult: Mix the culture into the drop until an even (homogeneous) suspension
steps.coverResult: Cover the drop with the cover slip (lowered on edge, no bubbles) and blot the excess with filter paper
steps.microResult: Examine the prep with the ×40 objective and determine motility

score.loopPartial: The loop was used without sterilising
score.mixZero: The culture was not mixed into the drop
score.coverPartial: The excess fluid was not blotted with filter paper
score.microZero: The prep was not viewed under the microscope

micro.question: Is this bacterium motile?
micro.revealMotile: The bacterium swims actively and directionally — this is true motility (via flagella), different from Brownian motion.
micro.revealNonmotile: The bacterium stays in place with only Brownian jitter — it is a non-motile species.

hint.dish: Place the nutrient Petri dish on the bench
hint.charge: Dip the sterile cotton swab into the E. coli culture
hint.dry: Let the surface dry ~5 minutes until absorbed
hint.forceps: Dip the forceps in the alcohol jar and flame-sterilise
hint.pickDisk: With the forceps, take an antibiotic disk from the set
hint.placeDisk: Place the disk held in the forceps onto the agar
hint.incubate: Invert the dish and place it in the incubator (37°C, 24 h)
hint.measure: Measure the inhibition zones and determine sensitivity

toast.needLawn: First swab the lawn, dry it and place all the disks

dry.title: The dish is drying…
dry.remain: Left: {m} min · the inoculum soaks into the agar

steps.lawnResult: Dip the sterile swab in the culture, rotate the dish ~60° and swab a «lawn» in 3 directions, then dry ~5 min
steps.disksResult: Place the antibiotic-impregnated paper disks onto the agar with forceps
steps.incubateResult: Keep the inoculated dish inverted at 37°C in the thermostat for 24 h
steps.measureResult: Measure the diameter of the clear (no-growth) zone around the disk
steps.classifyResult: Interpret the zone with the antibiotic breakpoints: Susceptible / Intermediate / Resistant (CLSI)

score.lawnIncomplete: The lawn was incomplete (3 directions needed)
score.notDried: The dish was not dried ~5 min
score.disksIncomplete: Not all the disks were placed
score.incubateZero: Not placed in the thermostat (no 24 h incubation)
score.measureIncomplete: Not all the disks were assessed

measure.title: Measure the inhibition zone
measure.instruction: Measure the clear-zone diameter around each disk and judge it by each antibiotic's own breakpoints: Susceptible (≥ S) / Intermediate / Resistant (≤ R).

gram.question: Which group does this bacterium belong to?
gram.revealPositive: This is a Gram-positive bacterium — it retains the crystal-violet–iodine complex and stays violet.
gram.revealNegative: This is a Gram-negative bacterium — it decolorizes in alcohol and is counterstained red by fuchsin.

hint.dishes: Place 3 nutrient Petri dishes on the bench
hint.loadPipette: Draw from the suspension with the pipette
hint.dropMaterial: Drop the pipette material onto dish 1
hint.dipSpatula: Dip the spreader tip into the alcohol jar
hint.flameSpatula: Flame the spreader — the alcohol burns off (then cool it)
hint.spread1: Spread onto the agar of dish 1 with the cooled sterile spreader
hint.spread2: With the same spreader (without re-sterilising) spread onto dish 2
hint.spread3: With the same spreader spread onto dish 3
hint.incubate: Place all 3 dishes in the thermostat (18–24 h)
hint.pickColony: Pick a single colony from dish 3 with the loop
hint.smear: Make a smear by running the loop over the slide
hint.micro: Take the slide to the microscope and read the result
hint.strikeMatch: Strike the match on the box
hint.lightLamp: Light the spirit lamp with the burning match
hint.disinfect: Drop the used spreader into the 5% chlorine disinfectant
hint.done: Done — the plates are out of the thermostat, work complete

toast.needBridge: First place the staining bridge on the bench

steps.dishesResult: Take 3 Petri dishes with nutrient medium
steps.spread1Result: Add material to dish 1 with the pipette and spread it onto the agar with the spreader
steps.spread23Result: With the same spreader (without re-sterilising) spread onto dishes 2 and 3, then incubate
steps.colonyResult: Take a pure culture from a single colony, make a smear and Gram-stain it
steps.microResult: View the smear under the microscope and determine the morphological and tinctorial properties
steps.sterilizeResult: Light the spirit lamp, dip the spreader in alcohol and flame it sterile
steps.disinfectResult: Drop the used spreader into 5% chlorine solution to decontaminate it
steps.incubateResult: Incubate the 3 plates at 37 °C for 18-24 h

score.spatulaNotSterile: The spreader was not sterilised beforehand
score.noMaterial: The test material was not added
score.incubateZero: Not placed in the thermostat (no incubation)
score.respread: The spreader was re-sterilised before dishes 2–3 — the dilution is broken
score.colonyZero: No colony was picked / no smear was made
score.gramIncomplete: The Gram stain was not completed (4 reagents: crystal violet, Lugol, alcohol, fuchsin)
score.gramWrong: The Gram type was determined incorrectly
score.noAlcohol: The spreader was not dipped in alcohol first
score.disinfectZero: The used spreader was not placed in 5% chlorine (no disinfection)
score.disinfectEarly: The spreader was disinfected before spreading
score.incubatePartial: Placed in the thermostat, but not all 3 plates were spread

result.subtitle: Drigalski method — exam result (PDF criteria)
result.right: The Gram type was determined correctly
result.wrong: The Gram type was determined incorrectly

hint.onBridge: Place the prepared smear (slide) on the staining bridge
hint.needFilter: Place filter paper over the smear
hint.gvApply: Drip crystal violet over the filter paper
hint.gvRemove: After 1–2 min, lift the filter paper with the forceps (the dye drains off)
hint.alcohol: Decolorize with ethyl alcohol (until the violet disappears)
hint.micro: Take the slide to the microscope and determine the Gram type

toast.needBridge: First place the staining bridge on the bench

steps.gvResult: Crystal violet (over filter paper) — 1–2 min, remove the paper and drain the dye
steps.lugolResult: Treat with Lugol's solution (1–2 min), drain the dye
steps.alcoholResult: Decolorize with ethyl alcohol (30–60 s) and wash with water
steps.fuchsinResult: Counterstain with fuchsin (1–2 min), wash and blot dry
steps.classifyResult: Determine the Gram type under the microscope

score.gvZero: Crystal violet was not applied at all
score.noFilter: Filter paper was not used (the dye must be dripped over the paper)
score.filterNotRemoved: The filter paper was not removed / the dye was not drained
score.gvTooEarly: Did not wait 1–2 min (removed too early)
score.lugolOrder: Wrong order (must come after crystal violet)
score.lugolTooShort: Lugol was not held long enough (1–2 min)
score.noWashAfterAlcohol: Not washed with water after alcohol
score.alcoholTooLong: Too long — over-decolorized (loses the Gram+ colour)
score.fuchsinTooShort: Fuchsin was not held long enough (1–2 min)
score.notBlotted: Not blotted dry with filter paper

result.subtitle: Gram staining — exam result (PDF criteria)
result.right: The Gram type was determined correctly
result.wrong: The Gram type was determined incorrectly

hint.strikeMatch: Strike the match on the matchbox
hint.lightLamp: Bring the burning match to the lamp
hint.discardMatch: Discard the match into the biohazard bin
hint.slideOnBridge: Place the slide on the staining bridge
hint.cleanSlide: Wipe the slide with the alcohol pad
hint.sterilizeLoop: Heat the loop in the flame — first vertical, then horizontal (rotate with the ↻ button)
hint.takeSample: Once the loop has cooled a little, take a sample from the culture (a hot loop kills the bacteria)
hint.smear: Run the loop over the slide to make a smear
hint.resterilize: Re-heat the loop in the flame (vertical + horizontal)
hint.airDry: Lift the slide and air-dry it (~12 s)
hint.fix: Pass the slide through the flame 3 times — heat fixation ({n}/3)
hint.applyMb: Bring methylene blue to the slide
hint.blot: Press the filter paper onto the slide (don't rub)
hint.toMicroscope: Take the slide to the microscope

lab1.extinguish: Put out the lamp with the cap

toast.dyeWait: The dye is still developing — wait
toast.needBridge: First place the staining bridge on the bench

micro.incomplete1: The preparation is incomplete (smear / fixation / staining missing).
micro.incomplete2: Almost no cells are visible under the microscope.
micro.note1: All cells are stained the same blue — a simple stain (methylene blue).
micro.note2: Visible shapes: cocci (in clusters) and rods (bacilli).
micro.noOil: Without immersion oil the 100× image is blurry — no oil added.
micro.high: With immersion oil the 100× image is sharp. Excellent preparation quality.
micro.medium: The image is satisfactory, but there are minor preparation flaws.
micro.low: Low quality — review the protocol steps.

steps.cleanSlideResult: Clean the slide with an alcohol pad
steps.addNaclResult: Place a drop of NaCl on the slide
steps.sterilizeLoopResult: Sterilise the loop in the flame
steps.takeSampleResult: Take a sample from the culture
steps.smearSampleResult: Make a smear (spread in the NaCl)
steps.flameFixResult: Heat-fix by passing through the flame
steps.applyMbResult: Flood and stain with methylene blue

detail.discardMatch: Discard the used match into the biohazard bin
detail.loopCooled: Cool the loop after sterilising before sampling
detail.waitedWash: Wait long enough after staining before washing (not too early)
detail.dryBeforeStain: Stain only after the slide is dry (not wet)
detail.loopReturned: Return the loop to its stand after use
detail.tubeReturned: Return the tube to its rack after use
detail.lampExtinguished: Put the lamp out with the cap at the end
detail.fixNotOverheated: Heat-fix by passing through the flame without holding (no overheating)
